content: Supabase RLS multi-tenant pattern: add owner_id/store_id column to every tenant-owned table, policies compare column to auth.uid() (or JWT claim) for authenticated writes, and a separate permissive SELECT policy grants anon/public role read access to a restricted column set (e.g. only published products of active stores) for the public storefront route. Without RLS enabled, the anon key can read/write all rows in exposed tables — RLS must be enabled explicitly per table (docs.supabase.com/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security).
